snippet: A school may engage in various trading operations as a service to the school community and/or to supplement school funding. The more common types of trading operations include canteen, out of school hours’ care and kindergartens. School councils must approve all school trading operations and all approvals must be recorded in the school council minutes. If a trading operation is to be outsourced (for example, out of school hours’ care and canteen licence), the school council must contact Legal Services Division External Link to determine the most appropriate outsourcing agreement. Legal Services can be contacted at legal.services@education.vic.gov.au or on 9637 3146 . School council must nominate and minute annually the taxation treatment for each trading operation. The taxation treatment (taxable or input taxed) applies to all transactions for that trading activity, revenue, expenditure and asset acquisition. The school council or the finance committee must review the viability of trading operations on a regular basis.
